context.project_description: Create token tracking infrastructure for LLM providers to monitor usage across chat and embedding operations, integrating with existing instrumentation systems.
key_requirements: Implement token counting mechanism that intercepts calls to LLM provider methods (chat, embed_batch)
Integrate with existing InstrumentedProxy system for wrapping providers without changing interfaces
Extract actual tokens from API responses where available (e.g., OpenAI response.usage) or estimate for other providers
Provide centralized token tracking and reporting capabilities for queries/sessions
constraints: Must leverage existing InstrumentedProxy pattern from fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
Token counting must be optional and configurable, not mandatory behavior
Should only track completion requests (chat) and embedding calls by default unless specifically requested
Must maintain backward compatibility with all existing LLM provider interfaces
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py provides InstrumentedProxy for wrapping objects; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines protocols for ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, etc.; fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py contain concrete implementations
stakeholders: LLM provider integrator who needs to instrument multiple LLM services
System architect responsible for maintaining backward compatibility while adding telemetry
Platform developer tasked with implementing token usage tracking without breaking existing functionality

in_scope: tracking token usage for chat completion requests
implementing token counting via InstrumentedProxy pattern
exposing token metrics through logging and SDK responses
out_of_scope: tracking embeddings or reranking token usage unless specifically requested
token estimation for providers that don't return explicit token counts

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/providers/token_counter.py — A base provider for tracking token usage across different LLM providers
fitz_ai/core/token_tracker.py — Centralized token tracking and reporting module

assumptions.assumption: We're only interested in counting LLM tokens used for completion requests (chat) and embedding calls; not reranking/vision unless specifically requested.
assumptions.impact: May need to add tracking for all provider methods if other use cases emerge

assumptions.assumption: The existing `InstrumentedProxy` system is sufficient to wrap providers without changing their interface significantly.
assumptions.impact: Would require refactoring the instrumentation architecture if it's not flexible enough

assumptions.assumption: Token counting is optional and should be enabled/disabled via configuration, not mandatory
assumptions.impact: Should be built in by default with no toggle if required for compliance or monitoring

assumptions.assumption: The core system already has access to token counts from the API responses (OpenAI provides this explicitly)
assumptions.impact: Need to build estimation logic for providers that don't return tokens, adding complexity and potential inaccuracies

assumptions.assumption: Per-query granularity is sufficient, no need for per-request or real-time tracking
assumptions.impact: Would require streaming/metric aggregation systems if more granular tracking becomes necessary

assumptions.assumption: Token counting should be additive to existing functionality and not change the behavior of LLM calls
assumptions.impact: May break compatibility or affect performance if it introduces overhead or alters call semantics

approaches.name: Incremental Answer Extension
approaches.description: Add token_usage field to existing Answer class with optional None default for backward compatibility
cons: Does not capture actual usage data from LLM providers
Only provides structure for future integration
best_for: Quick initial step that doesn't break existing APIs

approaches.name: Protocol-Based Refactoring with Structured Returns
approaches.description: Create new token-aware protocols and modify all LLM provider implementations to return structured responses including usage data
pros: Provides complete end-to-end token tracking capability
cons: High complexity due to interface changes across multiple files
Risk of breaking existing code without careful migration
best_for: Full architectural solution requiring comprehensive redesign

architecture.reasoning: The Incremental Answer Extension approach is recommended because it directly addresses the first identified factual error: the Answer class lacking a token_usage field. This approach introduces only the minimal necessary change (adding optional token_usage field) without disrupting existing behavior, making it safe for immediate implementation while preserving backward compatibility. The Protocol-Based Refactoring approach, though more complete and architecturally sound, is unnecessarily complex at this stage since it requires changing all LLM provider interfaces to return structured data rather than simple types as they currently do. Additionally, the reasoning correctly identifies that even with protocol changes, actual token usage extraction from API responses would require instrumentation system enhancements.

key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_functionality: Adding token_usage fields and changing LLM provider interfaces to return structured results breaks existing contracts, requiring phased migration to maintain backward compatibility while enabling new functionality.
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_scope: Extending instrumentation to capture token usage requires either protocol changes or manual wrapping; the current wrapper pattern doesn't automatically extract metadata from API responses without explicit implementation.

technology_considerations: Python dataclasses for structured Answer object extension with optional token_usage field
Protocol-based design for LLM provider interfaces to enable polymorphic behavior and backward-compatible evolution
Instrumentation system enhancement using wrapper pattern to intercept and aggregate usage data from API responses
API schema updates with optional token_usage fields in QueryResponse and ChatResponse models for client visibility
architecture.scope_statement: Extend Answer class in fitz_ai/core/answer.py with optional token_usage field and update related API response models to optionally include this data.

adrs.title: ADR: Extend Answer Class with Token Usage Field
adrs.context: The requested feature to track token usage for LLM queries requires modification to the core Answer dataclass to store structured metadata about token consumption.
adrs.decision: Extend the Answer class in fitz_ai/core/answer.py to include an optional token_usage field that can hold dictionary mapping token types to counts.
adrs.rationale: This is a minimal change that maintains backward compatibility while enabling future tracking of LLM usage. The field defaults to None for existing code paths and only gets populated when token usage data is available from LLM providers.
consequences: Backward compatible: Existing code that creates Answer instances without token_usage will continue working unchanged
New capability enabled: When LLM provider interfaces are updated, they can populate this field with actual token counts
API surface expansion: The Answer class now supports richer metadata for observability and cost tracking
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Modify Answer to always require token_usage field – rejected because it would break all existing code that creates Answer objects without usage data
Alternative 2 - Create a separate UsageAnswer class – rejected because it adds unnecessary complexity and breaks the paradigm of Answer being the single answer representation

adrs.title: ADR: Update LLM Provider Protocols to Return Structured Results with Metadata
adrs.context: Current LLM provider interfaces (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, VisionProvider) return simple types like str or list[float], making it impossible to propagate token usage metadata.
adrs.decision: Create new protocol interfaces that support structured return values including metadata for token consumption and other metrics. Update concrete implementations accordingly.
adrs.rationale: To enable proper token tracking, we must allow LLM providers to return structured data containing both the primary result (content) plus any associated metadata like usage statistics. This requires extending protocols without breaking existing code through interface evolution patterns.
consequences: Interface contract changes: All current implementations will need updates or new protocol implementations
Backward compatibility support required: Must provide legacy interfaces alongside new ones for migration period
Instrumentation system enhancement needed: Instrumentation must be able to extract and aggregate token usage data from structured responses
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Modify existing protocols in-place – rejected because it would break all current implementations immediately
Alternative 2 - Add optional metadata parameter to method signatures – rejected because Python doesn't support optional parameters in Protocol definitions and this wouldn't be idiomatic

adrs.title: ADR: Enhance Instrumentation System for Token Usage Extraction
adrs.context: The existing instrumentation system tracks performance timing but does not extract or aggregate token usage information from API responses.
adrs.decision: Extend the instrumentation system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py to capture and collect metadata including token usage when available through new wrapper logic.
adrs.rationale: The core architectural requirement is to gather aggregated usage metrics across all LLM calls within a query execution. The existing proxy pattern supports this but needs enhancement to specifically look for token data in structured responses from updated providers.
consequences: Enhanced observability: Enables tracking of aggregate token consumption per request
Instrumentation overhead consideration: Must be efficient and not impact performance significantly
New dependency on structured result types: Instrumented methods must return consistent metadata format
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Build separate metrics collection system – rejected because it duplicates functionality already present in instrumentation framework with more complexity
Alternative 2 - Manual injection at each LLM call site – rejected because it's error-prone, hard to maintain and violates DRY principles

adrs.title: ADR: Update API Response Models to Include Optional Token Usage Fields
adrs.context: API response models in fitz_ai/api/routes/query.py do not currently include fields for optional token usage data that might be available from the engine.
adrs.decision: Extend QueryResponse and ChatResponse models (or create new versions) to optionally include a token_usage field alongside the existing answer payload.
adrs.rationale: For end users to see cost information in API responses, we must update schema definitions. This follows standard REST patterns where optional metrics are included with responses when available without breaking compatibility for clients not expecting them.
consequences: Client flexibility: API consumers can access token usage if present or ignore it if not
Schema evolution required: New response models may be needed to maintain backward compatibility in versioned APIs
Serialization impact: Need to ensure None values are handled gracefully in JSON output
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Add token_usage as header field – rejected because headers aren't appropriate for structured data and don't integrate well with existing response model patterns
Alternative 2 - Make token_usage required in responses – rejected because it forces all APIs to compute and return usage even when not needed or available

adrs.title: ADR: Refactor Engine Layer for Aggregated Token Usage Calculation
adrs.context: The FitzKragEngine needs logic to accumulate and attach token usage data from individual LLM calls into the final Answer object.
adrs.decision: Modify answer() method in fitz_ai/engines/fitz_krag/engine.py to aggregate usage statistics across all LLM operations performed during query execution.
adrs.rationale: Since token tracking requires coordination between multiple pipeline stages and provider calls, the engine layer should act as an aggregation point for these metrics before returning final Answer objects. This provides a centralized place where complete usage information can be assembled.
consequences: Engine responsibility expansion: Engine now handles metadata collection in addition to core logic
Pipeline integration requirement: All LLM provider calls must return structured results for this to work correctly
Consistent aggregation pattern: Enables uniform token tracking across all query types and workflows
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 - Leave usage tracking to client SDK layer – rejected because it would be inconsistent, fragmented and harder to ensure completeness of metrics
Alternative 2 - Instrument each individual LLM call separately – rejected because this leads to duplicated logic and poor abstraction

components.name: Answer
responsibilities: Store text response from LLM queries

components.name: ChatProvider
responsibilities: Provide interface for sending messages to an LLM and receiving string responses

components.name: EmbeddingProvider
components.purpose: Protocol for generating embeddings from text
responsibilities: Generate vector representations of text inputs

components.name: VisionProvider
components.purpose: Protocol for image description using vision models
responsibilities: Describe images using base64 encoded strings and optional prompts
interfaces: describe_image(image_base64: str, prompt: str | None) -> str

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper for instrumenting method calls with benchmark hooks
responsibilities: Wrap target objects to track performance timing and allow hook registration
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: FitzKragEngine
responsibilities: Process queries through a multi-stage pipeline including ingestion, retrieval, and generation phases
interfaces: point(source: Path, collection: str | None) -> Any

components.name: fitz
components.purpose: Stateful SDK for the Fitz KRAG framework
responsibilities: Provide high-level interface to interact with collections and ask questions
interfaces: __init__(collection: str, config_path: Optional[Union[str, Path]], auto_init: bool) -> None
ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

components.name: BenchmarkHook
responsibilities: Define interface for tracking method call start and end events with timing data
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

components.name: RerankProvider
components.purpose: Protocol for re-ranking documents based on query relevance
responsibilities: Rank a list of documents according to their relevance to the given query
interfaces: rerank(query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]

artifacts.purpose: Extends the Answer class to include optional token_usage field for tracking LLM consumption metrics
Defines new token-aware LLM provider protocols that return structured results with metadata
Enhances instrumentation system to track and aggregate token usage data from LLM provider calls

artifacts.content:
# This is a hypothetical file path since the actual schemas are in fitz_ai/api/models/schemas.py
# but we must create it with required fields based on the analysis.

class QueryResponse:
    answer: Answer
    token_usage: dict[str, int] | None = None  # NEW FIELD

class ChatResponse:
    answer: Answer  
    token_usage: dict[str, int] | None = None  # NEW FIELD
artifacts.purpose: Updates API response models to optionally include token usage information in responses

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Instrumentation System Design
phases.objective: Design a flexible instrumentation system that allows benchmarking and caching of LLM provider methods with minimal boilerplate
key_risks: Method signature mismatch during proxy wrapping
Performance overhead from extensive wrapping
Thread safety issues in hook registration

phases.name: LLM Provider Instrumentation Integration
phases.objective: Integrate the instrumentation system with existing LLM providers to enable performance monitoring and caching
key_risks: Breaking changes in method signatures across provider implementations
Inconsistent performance characteristics between different providers
Caching invalidation strategies for dynamic content

phases.name: Benchmarking Hook Implementation
phases.objective: Implement concrete benchmarking hooks to collect performance data from instrumented LLM calls
key_risks: High overhead from timing measurements affecting actual performance
Data race conditions in concurrent benchmark collection
Storage limitations for large-scale benchmark datasets

phases.name: Caching Hook Implementation
phases.objective: Implement caching hooks to reduce redundant LLM calls and improve response times
deliverables: Cache key generation mechanism based on method args/kwargs
In-memory cache implementation with TTL support
Persistent cache adapter for database storage
key_risks: Cache consistency issues with concurrent updates
Memory leaks from unbounded cache growth
Serialization/deserialization problems for complex return types

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
